candidate_programs.title: Multimodal And Embodied Small Models
candidate_programs.focus: Test whether structured execution and evidence-selection patterns survive when inputs include images, UI state, audio, or physical observations.

candidate_programs.title: Data Generation And Synthetic Curriculum Design
candidate_programs.focus: Learn which synthetic curricula create transfer rather than benchmark-specific competence.

candidate_programs.title: Small-Model Collaboration
candidate_programs.focus: Measure whether multiple specialized small models outperform one single-policy small model under fixed budget.

candidate_programs.title: On-Device And Latency-Constrained Agents
candidate_programs.focus: Find mechanisms that preserve gains under strict inference, memory, latency, and tool-call ceilings.

candidate_programs.title: Human-Agent Research Interfaces
candidate_programs.focus: Build interfaces that help people and agents inspect, branch, and critique experiment evidence quickly.

proposals.title: Visible Abstention Selector Benchmark
proposals.question: Can a selector improve precision without hiding failures behind an unreported commit-rate drop?
proposals.hypothesis: Explicit abstention labels and coverage reporting will separate useful evidence selection from silent cherry-picking.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Build one shared candidate pool with visible-only features, train selector variants, and report precision, recall, coverage, and selected accuracy by family.
proposals.success_signal: A visible-only selector beats first-visible, shortest-visible, and random-order baselines at matched coverage.
proposals.failure_signal: Accuracy gains disappear when coverage is matched or family-held-out evaluation is applied.
proposals.next_step: Run idea intake against selector and oracle-ceiling anchor experiments.
proposals.avoid: Do not use hidden expected answers as features or training labels unless clearly reported as an oracle ceiling.

proposals.title: Expected-Output-Free Counterexample Tests
proposals.question: Can acquired tests distinguish candidates without requiring hidden expected outputs?
proposals.hypothesis: Agreement, invariants, metamorphic checks, and execution consistency can improve selection while preserving deployability.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Generate candidate-specific tests with no hidden answer key, compare by invariants and cross-candidate agreement, then evaluate selected outputs only after commit.
proposals.success_signal: Selection improves over random acquisition and fixed-order acquisition under the same evidence budget.
proposals.failure_signal: The method helps only when hidden answers or task-specific leaks enter the evidence loop.
proposals.next_step: Start from active counterexample and model-in-loop counterexample experiments.
proposals.avoid: Do not treat post-hoc oracle scoring as deployable evidence.

proposals.title: Independent Consensus Versus Selector Pool
proposals.question: When does independent implementation consensus beat a trained selector on the same candidate pool?
proposals.hypothesis: Consensus helps when failures are uncorrelated, while learned selectors help when visible evidence is structured but sparse.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Create matched candidate pools with independent generation, learned selector scores, and consensus scores; evaluate under family holdouts.
proposals.success_signal: The comparison identifies regimes where consensus, selection, or hybrid routing dominates.
proposals.failure_signal: Consensus gains vanish after controlling for extra samples or shared prompt artifacts.
proposals.next_step: Use existing independent retrieval and counterexample selection anchors as prior evidence.
proposals.avoid: Do not compare methods with unequal sample budgets.

proposals.title: Deployable Gap Scorecards
proposals.question: Which oracle-ceiling results remain promising after visible-only constraints are made explicit?
proposals.hypothesis: A standard scorecard will reveal reusable mechanisms and retire variants that depend on non-deployable evidence.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Select high-impact oracle-ceiling experiments, annotate evidence visibility, baselines, artifact location, and deployable follow-up.
proposals.success_signal: Every reviewed ceiling result has a clear keep, rerun, or retire decision.
proposals.failure_signal: Scorecards cannot distinguish mechanism promise from hidden-label dependence.
proposals.next_step: Seed from selector, code ABI, and coverage-gate reports.
proposals.avoid: Do not rewrite old results as deployable when they were explicitly oracle-bounded.

proposals.title: Typed Versus Latent Versus Text Compiler Suite
proposals.question: Which structured representation causes generalization: typed bytecode, latent slots, text programs, or execution feedback?
proposals.hypothesis: Typed state helps most under compositional shifts, while latent state helps only when supervision and task families align.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Run direct text, typed bytecode, latent slot, and execution-feedback variants on one shared suite with length, family, and paraphrase shifts.
proposals.success_signal: At least one representation has a reproducible advantage across non-IID splits and matched budget.
proposals.failure_signal: All gains collapse under shared splits or shuffled/corrupted state controls.
proposals.next_step: Use compiler, latent executor, and structured slot anchors to define the suite.
proposals.avoid: Do not compare representations on different task mixes or budgets.

proposals.title: Compiler Shift Replication Grid
proposals.question: Which compiler-style gains survive seed, length, operator, and paraphrase shifts?
proposals.hypothesis: Some strong results are robust to seed changes but brittle to family and paraphrase shifts.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Replicate top compiler-style experiments across a small grid of seeds and shifts with identical reporting.
proposals.success_signal: The grid identifies stable effects and brittle effects without requiring a new mechanism.
proposals.failure_signal: Replication variance overwhelms claimed mechanism differences.
proposals.next_step: Pick three anchor experiments from the compiler program scorecard.
proposals.avoid: Do not add a new model variant until the shift grid is defined.

proposals.title: Supervision Causality Ablation
proposals.question: Is the lift from state-prefix supervision, final-answer supervision, program-token supervision, or data filtering?
proposals.hypothesis: State supervision contributes only when state variables map cleanly to task factors.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Run matched ablations with final-answer only, state-prefix, program-token, shuffled-state, and corrupted-state supervision.
proposals.success_signal: A named supervision source survives shuffled and corrupted controls.
proposals.failure_signal: Lift tracks data filtering or token budget rather than the intended representation.
proposals.next_step: Start from structured slot and dense supervision ladder anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not infer causality from a single no-control training run.

proposals.title: Memory Mode Comparison Suite
proposals.question: Which memory use is actually useful: prompt context, constraints, tests, candidates, or failure cases?
proposals.hypothesis: Memory is most useful when converted into constraints or tests rather than appended as context.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Retrieve the same source items, transform them into multiple memory modes, and evaluate on matched held-out families.
proposals.success_signal: Constraint or test memory beats prompt memory, corrupted retrieval, and no-memory baselines.
proposals.failure_signal: All memory modes underperform direct inference or rely on same-family leakage.
proposals.next_step: Use independent retrieval and feature-factorized rule experiments as anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not treat larger prompts as a free budget increase.

proposals.title: Constraint Memory Counterexample Generator
proposals.question: Can retrieved failures become counterexample generators instead of answer hints?
proposals.hypothesis: Failure-case memory can create discriminating tests while reducing direct-answer contamination.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Retrieve similar verified failures, extract constraints, generate candidate-specific counterexamples, and evaluate selection impact.
proposals.success_signal: Generated counterexamples improve selection under visible-only constraints.
proposals.failure_signal: Counterexamples are either redundant with public tests or leak hidden labels.
proposals.next_step: Run related-work discovery for counterexample memory and active acquisition.
proposals.avoid: Do not copy expected outputs from retrieved examples into the new task.

proposals.title: Operator Card Schema Probe
proposals.question: What operator-card fields make inventories reusable across experiments?
proposals.hypothesis: Examples, invariants, aliases, failure modes, and type signatures are enough for shortlisting and debugging.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Define a small card schema, encode one existing operator bank, and test retrieval and shortlisting against a baseline bank.
proposals.success_signal: Cards improve diagnosis or top-k operator retrieval without increasing manual ambiguity.
proposals.failure_signal: The schema is too heavy or fails to support held-out operator lookup.
proposals.next_step: Sample operator inventory experiments and extract common fields.
proposals.avoid: Do not create a schema that only fits one existing task family.

proposals.title: Operator Bank Shortlister Scaling
proposals.question: Can a shortlister preserve selected accuracy as operator banks grow?
proposals.hypothesis: A learned top-k shortlister will beat random and heuristic search when trained with held-out primitive families.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Scale bank size, train top-k shortlisters, compare full-search oracle, random shortlister, and closed-vocabulary baselines.
proposals.success_signal: Top-k cost falls while deployable selected accuracy stays above heuristic baselines.
proposals.failure_signal: Coverage rises but selected accuracy drops without a reliable selection plan.
proposals.next_step: Use inventory shortlister and scaling-stress experiments as anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not report coverage without selected accuracy and search cost.

proposals.title: Active Disambiguation For Type-Colliding Operators
proposals.question: Can active probes separate operators that share type signatures but differ semantically?
proposals.hypothesis: A few targeted examples can disambiguate type-colliding operators better than passive shortlist scores.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Construct operator pairs with shared signatures, ask an acquisition policy for distinguishing probes, and evaluate downstream operator choice.
proposals.success_signal: Targeted probes improve operator selection per evidence token over random probes.
proposals.failure_signal: Disambiguation requires hidden outputs or more budget than full search.
proposals.next_step: Identify collisions from current operator inventory runs.
proposals.avoid: Do not count probes that reveal the hidden target operator directly.

proposals.title: Acquisition Policy Comparison Pool
proposals.question: Which acquisition rule wins when uncertainty, disagreement, information gain, and learned policies share one pool?
proposals.hypothesis: Learned policies help only when trained on features that transfer across task families.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Build one acquisition pool and run random, diversity, disagreement, information gain, and learned policies with fixed budgets.
proposals.success_signal: A policy improves selection quality per unit evidence across at least two families.
proposals.failure_signal: The best policy changes unpredictably across families or loses to fixed-order acquisition.
proposals.next_step: Derive pool features from active trace policy and adaptive evidence budget experiments.
proposals.avoid: Do not compare policies on different candidate or evidence pools.

proposals.title: Family-Aware Evidence Policy
proposals.question: Can acquisition policies adapt to date/time, numeric, table, and code task families without overfitting?
proposals.hypothesis: Family-aware features improve evidence value, but only when family labels are coarse and deployable.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Train policy variants with no family, coarse family, and oracle family features; evaluate on held-out family mixtures.
proposals.success_signal: Coarse deployable family features improve budget-normalized selection over no-family controls.
proposals.failure_signal: Only oracle family labels help or performance collapses on held-out families.
proposals.next_step: Use current active evidence experiments to define feature families.
proposals.avoid: Do not use benchmark split identity as a deployable feature.

proposals.title: STOP/MORE Controller With Visible Labels
proposals.question: Can a controller decide when to gather more evidence using only visible training labels?
proposals.hypothesis: A STOP/MORE policy can beat fixed-budget and always-continue heuristics when trained on deployable features.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Define tool-state features, train STOP/MORE policies without hidden correctness labels at decision time, and compare against fixed budgets.
proposals.success_signal: The learned controller improves selected accuracy per cost under matched evidence constraints.
proposals.failure_signal: The controller only wins by using hidden labels or by silently spending more budget.
proposals.next_step: Anchor on adaptive tool controller and adaptive evidence budget policy experiments.
proposals.avoid: Do not let hidden evaluation correctness enter runtime state.

proposals.title: Noisy Tool Controller Stress Test
proposals.question: Do process policies still help when tools are flaky, delayed, or misleading?
proposals.hypothesis: Robust controllers should learn when to distrust or repeat tool evidence, not just call tools more often.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Inject controlled tool noise, latency, and misleading examples into an existing tool loop and compare learned, heuristic, and oracle policies.
proposals.success_signal: A controller preserves accuracy-cost tradeoffs under noise better than fixed and always-tool baselines.
proposals.failure_signal: The policy overuses noisy tools or transfers poorly across noise regimes.
proposals.next_step: Use process-control experiments with explicit tool states as anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not evaluate only on clean tools after training with noise.

proposals.title: Posttraining Method Shared Substrate
proposals.question: How do SFT, DPO, process distillation, and DAgger compare on one candidate/evidence substrate?
proposals.hypothesis: Method ranking changes when all methods share data, model, budget, controls, and held-out families.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Prepare one substrate, train method variants with frozen and shuffled-label controls, and evaluate on IID plus held-out splits.
proposals.success_signal: One method shows a robust advantage over frozen, shuffled, and weak-baseline controls.
proposals.failure_signal: All gains are explained by data selection, extra tokens, or weak baselines.
proposals.next_step: Use DPO, DAgger, and distillation experiments to define shared data.
proposals.avoid: Do not check adapters or model checkpoints into git.

proposals.title: Hard Negative Training Transfer
proposals.question: Does training on hard negatives improve real selection, or does it overfit visible failure artifacts?
proposals.hypothesis: Hard-negative training helps only when negatives represent held-out families and not just surface artifacts.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Train with mined hard negatives, shuffled negatives, and random negatives; evaluate coverage, selected accuracy, and false positives on held-out families.
proposals.success_signal: Mined negatives reduce false positives without lowering recall or transfer.
proposals.failure_signal: The model learns benchmark-specific artifacts and fails held-out negative families.
proposals.next_step: Start from offline hard-negative and constrained coverage DPO anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not report aggregate accuracy without false-positive slices.

proposals.title: Adapter-Free Reproducibility Manifest Audit
proposals.question: Can every trained-run experiment be audited without committing adapters or checkpoints?
proposals.hypothesis: A standardized manifest is enough to preserve provenance while keeping large artifacts external.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Audit experiments with training outputs, require external artifact pointers, model IDs, data hashes, and reproduction notes.
proposals.success_signal: All trained-run folders either have manifests or explicit no-large-artifact notes.
proposals.failure_signal: Important trained outputs cannot be located or reproduced without local private files.
proposals.next_step: Use artifact manifest index and readiness matrix to prioritize missing manifests.
proposals.avoid: Do not add model weights, adapters, or checkpoints to git.

proposals.title: Diagnostics Failure-Slicing Template
proposals.question: Which failure slices should every major experiment report before influencing strategy?
proposals.hypothesis: A small standard slice set will change follow-up decisions more often than ad hoc diagnostics.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Define slices by operator, family, length, parse status, evidence state, and artifact boundary; apply to three anchor reports.
proposals.success_signal: The slices reveal at least one concrete branch, stop, or rerun decision per anchor.
proposals.failure_signal: Slices are descriptive but do not change any experiment decision.
proposals.next_step: Apply to one compiler, one selector, and one posttraining anchor.
proposals.avoid: Do not add diagnostics without a decision they can change.

proposals.title: Pressure Diagnostic Preflight
proposals.question: Can cheap token-pressure and execution-pressure probes predict when an expensive run is worth doing?
proposals.hypothesis: Preflight probes can detect capacity or tool-pressure failures before full training or large sweeps.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Run tiny diagnostic probes on candidate mechanisms, compare their signals with later full-run results, and estimate decision precision.
proposals.success_signal: Preflight results correctly predict stop or proceed decisions on multiple mechanisms.
proposals.failure_signal: Preflight signals have no relation to full-run outcomes.
proposals.next_step: Use token-pressure and tool-control experiments as anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not treat preflight as evidence for final claims without full evaluation.

proposals.title: Hidden-Label Boundary Linter
proposals.question: Can repository checks force every new result to label oracle-only and deployable evidence boundaries?
proposals.hypothesis: A lightweight text and metadata linter can prevent hidden-label confusion without slowing pilots.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Add a required report section or metadata field, scan new experiments, and document accepted boundary labels.
proposals.success_signal: New experiments fail validation when hidden-label boundary documentation is missing.
proposals.failure_signal: The rule creates noisy failures or misses obvious oracle/deployable ambiguity.
proposals.next_step: Prototype against high-impact selector and oracle-ceiling reports.
proposals.avoid: Do not require heavy formal proof for early pilots.

proposals.title: Reproducibility Scorecards For High-Impact Experiments
proposals.question: Which high-impact experiments are reproducible enough to guide future programs?
proposals.hypothesis: A scorecard over seeds, data, commands, artifacts, and boundary labels will prioritize curation work.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Score top anchor experiments from claims and program scorecards, then add curation tasks for missing pieces.
proposals.success_signal: Each high-impact anchor has a reproducibility grade and concrete repair task.
proposals.failure_signal: Scorecards duplicate the readiness matrix without adding decision value.
proposals.next_step: Use experiment readiness and claim evidence counts to select anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not block exploratory work on full reproducibility unless it is used as shared evidence.

proposals.title: Agent Evidence Navigation Test
proposals.question: Can a new agent find relevant prior evidence faster using repository indexes than by raw search alone?
proposals.hypothesis: Program indexes, claims, readiness, and related-work search reduce duplicate proposals and improve anchor selection.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Give agents held-out rough ideas, compare raw rg search against documented discovery workflow, and score anchor quality and time.
proposals.success_signal: The documented workflow finds better anchors with lower duplicate rate.
proposals.failure_signal: Agents ignore or cannot use the generated knowledge surfaces.
proposals.next_step: Use existing discovery workflow and related-work CLI.
proposals.avoid: Do not evaluate only on ideas already named in file titles.

proposals.title: Proposal Deduplication Audit
proposals.question: Can the repo detect repeated experiment ideas before they become new runs?
proposals.hypothesis: A sample-based audit against catalog, claims, and future queue will catch most near duplicates.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Sample proposed ideas, run related-work search, compare against human duplicate judgments, and update intake requirements.
proposals.success_signal: The audit catches near duplicates without suppressing genuinely new variants.
proposals.failure_signal: Duplicate warnings are too noisy or miss obvious repeated ideas.
proposals.next_step: Generate cases from future queue items and recent scaffolded ideas.
proposals.avoid: Do not equate shared topic tags with duplicate mechanisms.

proposals.title: Multimodal Visual Table Transform Probe
proposals.question: Do executable intermediates help small models transform visual table inputs?
proposals.hypothesis: Structured intermediate programs will transfer better than direct vision-language answers on held-out visual table layouts.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Adapt one table-transform task to image-rendered tables, compare direct answer, extracted structure, and executable intermediate variants.
proposals.success_signal: Executable intermediates improve held-out layout accuracy without manual table extraction leakage.
proposals.failure_signal: The gain depends entirely on an external OCR or parser oracle.
proposals.next_step: Run intake using Foofah and table-transform anchors.
proposals.avoid: Do not hide vision parsing errors by evaluating only on gold extracted tables.

proposals.title: UI State Tool Execution Probe
proposals.question: Can process-control policies transfer from text/table tools to UI-state observations?
proposals.hypothesis: Tool-state schemas can represent UI observations and action history with only small extensions.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Create a tiny UI-state task suite, map observations into the existing tool-state schema, and compare heuristic versus learned controllers.
proposals.success_signal: The controller transfers basic STOP/MORE or repair behavior to UI tasks under a fixed budget.
proposals.failure_signal: UI state requires a new representation that invalidates the current process-control schema.
proposals.next_step: Prototype with static UI states before live browser control.
proposals.avoid: Do not start with unconstrained open-web tasks.

proposals.title: Synthetic Curriculum Transfer Bakeoff
proposals.question: Which synthetic curriculum source transfers best: human-designed, model-generated, or failure-mined?
proposals.hypothesis: Failure-mined curricula transfer better than broad model-generated curricula when held-out primitives are explicit.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Generate three curriculum sources for one held-out primitive suite and compare transfer under matched data volume and model budget.
proposals.success_signal: One curriculum source improves held-out performance without hurting direct baselines.
proposals.failure_signal: Curriculum gains are IID-only or disappear under matched difficulty.
proposals.next_step: Reuse bridge, recombination, and held-out primitive split patterns.
proposals.avoid: Do not judge curricula only on training or IID validation.

proposals.title: Failure-Mined Curriculum Generator
proposals.question: Can failure slices automatically generate new training cases that reduce repeated errors?
proposals.hypothesis: Failure-mined cases help when tied to a falsifiable slice and held-out control, not when used as unfiltered augmentation.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Mine common failure slices, generate synthetic cases, train or prompt with them, and evaluate on untouched held-out failures.
proposals.success_signal: The targeted slice improves without creating new false positives elsewhere.
proposals.failure_signal: Generated cases overfit surface patterns or degrade unrelated slices.
proposals.next_step: Start with diagnostic failure-slicing template once available.
proposals.avoid: Do not train on evaluation failures without a clean holdout.

proposals.title: Multi-Model Role Ensemble Pool
proposals.question: Can generator, verifier, critic, and evidence-acquirer roles outperform a single small-model policy under fixed total budget?
proposals.hypothesis: Role specialization helps only when roles contribute non-redundant evidence and share a strict cost accounting.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Assign roles on one candidate pool, compare single-policy, homogeneous ensemble, role ensemble, and oracle routing at equal cost.
proposals.success_signal: Role ensembles improve selected accuracy or coverage at matched budget.
proposals.failure_signal: All gains come from extra samples or correlated role outputs.
proposals.next_step: Use consensus and selector anchors to define the shared pool.
proposals.avoid: Do not compare a multi-role system to a weaker single-model budget.

proposals.title: Committee Verifier-Critic Loop
proposals.question: Can a committee decide when critique or verification is worth another step?
proposals.hypothesis: A small committee can learn budget-aware escalation better than fixed critique rounds.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Run fixed-round, single-controller, and committee-controller loops on identical pools with explicit action costs.
proposals.success_signal: Committee control beats fixed rounds and single-controller baselines on accuracy per cost.
proposals.failure_signal: Committee discussion increases cost without improving commit quality.
proposals.next_step: Start from STOP/MORE controller and consensus experiments.
proposals.avoid: Do not evaluate with unlimited debate rounds.

proposals.title: Latency-Ceiling Selector Rerun
proposals.question: Which selector or tool-control gains survive strict latency, token, and call ceilings?
proposals.hypothesis: Some evidence mechanisms remain useful when converted into early-exit and small-k policies.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Rerun one selector/tool-control experiment with strict latency, token, and tool-call budgets plus early-exit policies.
proposals.success_signal: A constrained policy retains a meaningful fraction of the unconstrained gain over simple baselines.
proposals.failure_signal: The method collapses when evidence budget is capped or action latency is counted.
proposals.next_step: Pick an existing adaptive controller with a clear smoke path.
proposals.avoid: Do not ignore preprocessing or tool-call latency.

proposals.title: Compressed Memory Budget Probe
proposals.question: Can memory remain useful when context and storage are severely compressed?
proposals.hypothesis: Constraint and test memories compress better than raw examples.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Compare raw-example, summary, constraint, and test-memory compression under fixed context and storage ceilings.
proposals.success_signal: Compressed constraint or test memory beats no-memory and raw-summary controls.
proposals.failure_signal: Compression removes the signal that made memory useful.
proposals.next_step: Use memory mode comparison suite once its schema exists.
proposals.avoid: Do not count retrieval time as free.

proposals.title: Static Research Navigation Dashboard
proposals.question: Can a static dashboard help humans and agents choose better next experiments than Markdown indexes alone?
proposals.hypothesis: A dashboard over programs, claims, readiness, artifacts, and future queue will reduce search time and duplicate proposals.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Build a static generated dashboard from existing CSV/JSON indexes and evaluate it with navigation tasks.
proposals.success_signal: Users or agents find higher-quality anchors faster than with raw Markdown navigation.
proposals.failure_signal: The dashboard duplicates existing docs without changing decisions.
proposals.next_step: Start with generated data files already produced by make catalog.
proposals.avoid: Do not require a server or external service for core navigation.

proposals.title: Evidence Branching UI Probe
proposals.question: What interface lets a researcher branch from one result into controls, replications, or new programs fastest?
proposals.hypothesis: Branch choices improve when claims, readiness gaps, related experiments, and program backlogs are visible in one workflow.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Prototype a static branching view for a small set of anchor experiments and compare branch quality with the current docs workflow.
proposals.success_signal: Branch proposals cite more relevant evidence and fewer near duplicates.
proposals.failure_signal: The interface adds navigation overhead without improving proposal quality.
proposals.next_step: Use high-impact claim evidence as the first anchor set.
proposals.avoid: Do not build an interface before defining the branch-quality rubric.

proposals.title: Learned Thinking-Budget Controller
proposals.question: Can a deployable controller allocate the thinking-token budget better than the fixed ~1024 optimum, given thinking has an overthinking cost?
proposals.hypothesis: A visible-signal STOP/MORE policy over thinking tokens can match or beat a fixed budget at lower mean cost by spending more thinking only on hard inputs.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Reuse the thinking_budget_scaling MBPP harness; learn or threshold a per-task budget from visible features; compare deployable accuracy-vs-mean-thinking-tokens against fixed budgets and unbudgeted.
proposals.success_signal: Controller Pareto-dominates fixed budgets on deployable accuracy vs mean thinking tokens, beating a length/difficulty-blind baseline.
proposals.failure_signal: No improvement over the best fixed budget once mean token cost is matched.
proposals.next_step: Anchor on qwen35_4b_thinking_budget_scaling and the evidence-budget controller experiments.
proposals.avoid: Do not let the controller read hidden test outcomes; budget decisions must use visible information only.

proposals.title: Thinking Content vs Compute (Stronger Control)
proposals.question: How much of the native-thinking gain is coherent reasoning versus compute + scaffold + token-presence?
proposals.hypothesis: Substituting a different task's thinking (removing token-presence, not just order) will collapse more of the gain than shuffling, isolating the genuine-reasoning share.
proposals.minimal_protocol: On the thinking_budget_scaling substrate, add controls: shuffled-thinking (token order), foreign-task thinking (token-presence removed), and matched-compute no-think; compare deployable accuracy at matched budgets.
proposals.success_signal: A clear ordering real > foreign > shuffled > no_think that quantifies the reasoning vs compute/scaffold split.
proposals.failure_signal: Foreign-task thinking matches real thinking, i.e. the gain is essentially compute + scaffold, not reasoning.
proposals.next_step: Extend qwen35_4b_thinking_budget_scaling controls with foreign-task thinking.
proposals.avoid: Do not infer a content effect from a single budget; sweep and report noise.

proposals.title: Gauntlet Round 3: Does Expert Iteration Compound On The Blackbox Instrument?
proposals.question: Re-harvesting the gym with the round-2 model: does the frontier open (glyphgate/loomfix/stallwright), does menagerie keep responding, and does iteration compound or re-saturate (C11 said coverage-bounded; the frontier moved in round 2)?
proposals.hypothesis: The round-2 model's collapsed truncation cascade raises harvest yield everywhere, opening the three starved families and buying a second menagerie step; compounding will be smaller than round 2's jump because the emission-seam repair is one-time.
proposals.minimal_protocol: Fast-profile re-harvest with the round-2 merged model (same seeds namespace, K=2), rebuild SFT with the round-2 recipe (recovery arm + weighted loss), train round-3 adapter from BASE on the union data, then paired quick events on two fresh seeds via the HF backend plus the recovery-arm-only and breadth-vs-matched-dose ablations.
proposals.success_signal: Round-3 harvest keepers in glyphgate/loomfix/stallwright at 3x round-1 levels; menagerie quick delta over base grows beyond round 2's +0.22/+0.29 mean on fresh paired seeds.
proposals.failure_signal: Yield rises but menagerie plateaus (emission-seam repair was the whole effect) or gym-internal gains stop transferring (locality reappears above the protocol layer).
expected_artifacts: ablation table separating recovery-arm from breadth effects
proposals.next_step: Run the fast-profile re-harvest with the round-2 merged model once the fla-kernel medium-tier fault on the host is triaged.
proposals.avoid: Never evaluate adapters through vLLM runtime LoRA (C49); never mix HF-backend and vLLM-backend scores; keep menagerie seeds fresh per event.
